kw.name: Register Keyword To Run On Failure
kw.msg: Capture Screenshot On Failure will be run on failure.
kw.doc: Sets the keyword to be executed when an AppiumLibrary keyword fails.

test.name: TC_M2.2_007 - Invalid OTP Shows Validation Error On FP OTP Screen

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
Close Application
Run Keyword And Return Status
Capture Page Screenshot
kw.doc: Takes a screenshot of the current page and embeds it into the log.
Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
Log
kw.msg: Could not capture screenshot — no active Appium session
kw.arg: Could not capture screenshot — no active Appium session
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.arg: Could not capture screenshot — no active Appium session
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.
Closes the current application and the webdriver session.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Open Application
kw.doc: Opens a new application to the given Appium server. Capabilities of appium server, Android and iOS, Please check https://appium.io/docs/en/2.19/guides/caps/ | *Option* | *Man.* | *Description* | | remote_url | Yes | Appium server url | | alias | No | alias |

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Run
kw.msg: Running command 'adb -s emulator-5554 shell pm clear com.higala.ruralbanksanantonioitg 2>&1'.

kw.name: Run
kw.msg: Running command 'adb -s emulator-5554 shell am force-stop com.higala.ruralbanksanantonioitg 2>&1'.

kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Activate Application
kw.doc: Activates the application if it is not running or is running in the background.

kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Clicks the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.TextView[contains(@text,'Invalid or has expired') or contains(@text,'OTP is either')]
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Expect Element
kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.TextView[contains(@text,'Invalid or has expired') or contains(@text,'OTP is either')]
kw.doc: Verifies that the element with the given ``locator`` has the desired ``state`` (visible, not visible, enabled, disabled.)

kw.name: Close Application
kw.doc: Closes the current application and the webdriver session.

test.doc: Verify that entering an incorrect OTP on the FP OTP screen shows a validation error (m2.2.9) Expected: "OTP is either Invalid or has expired, Please try again or request a new OTP"

test.name: TC_M2.2_009 - Fifth Failed OTP Attempt Across Multiple Resend Requests Triggers Max Attempts Error

test.doc: Verify that 5 total failed OTP attempts spanning multiple resend requests still triggers the max attempts error on the 5th attempt (m2.2.12) Flow: 2 wrong → resend → 2 wrong → resend → 1 wrong (5th) → max attempts error

test.name: TC_M2.2_020 - FP Email Screen Shows Validation For Invalid Email Format

test.doc: Verify that an incorrectly formatted email on FP screen shows "Incorrect email format" (m2.2.24 step 2)

test.name: TC_M2.2_021 - FP Email Screen Shows Validation For Unregistered Email

test.doc: Verify that an unregistered email on the FP screen shows an invalid email error (m2.2.24 step 3) Expected: "Email address is invalid, please try again."

test.name: TC_M2.2_023 - New Password Field Shows Complexity Validation For Invalid Password

test.doc: Verify that entering a password that doesn't meet complexity requirements on the New Password field shows the password complexity validation message (m2.2.26) Expected: "Please follow the password complexity: 1 Uppercase, 1 Lowercase, 1 Number, 1 Special Char, 8-127 chars"

test.name: TC_M2.2_024 - Confirm Password Field Shows Complexity Validation For Invalid Password

kw.name: Expect Element
kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.TextView[contains(@text,'password complexity') or contains(@text,'Please follow')]
kw.doc: Verifies that the element with the given ``locator`` has the desired ``state`` (visible, not visible, enabled, disabled.)

kw.name: Close Application
kw.doc: Closes the current application and the webdriver session.

test.doc: Verify that entering an invalid password in the Confirm Password field shows complexity validation (m2.2.27)

test.name: TC_M2.2_025 - New Password Screen Shows Error When Passwords Do Not Match

kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.TextView[contains(@text,'does not match') or contains(@text,'do not match') or contains(@text,'Passwords does not')]

kw.doc: Closes the current application and the webdriver session.

test.doc: Verify that entering mismatched passwords shows "Passwords do not match. Please try again." (m2.2.28)

test.name: TC_M2.2_032 - Verify Blocked FP Email Shows Error Message During Block Period

kw.msg: StaleElementReferenceException: Message: Cached elements 'By.xpath: //android.widget.EditText' do not exist in DOM anymore; For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#staleelementreferenceexception Stacktrace: io.appium.uiautomator2.common.exceptions.StaleElementReferenceException: Cached elements 'By.xpath: //android.widget.EditText' do not exist in DOM anymore at io.appium.uiautomator2.model.ElementsCache.restore(ElementsCache.java:78) at io.appium.uiautomator2.model.ElementsCache.get(ElementsCache.java:155) at io.appium.uiautomator2.handler.SendKeysToElement.safeHandle(SendKeysToElement.java:104) at io.appium.uiautomator2.handler.request.SafeRequestHandler.handle(SafeRequestHandler.java:59) at io.appium.uiautomator2.server.AppiumServlet.handleRequest(AppiumServlet.java:267) at io.appium.uiautomator2.server.AppiumServlet.handleHttpRequest(AppiumServlet.java:261) at io.appium.uiautomator2.http.ServerHandler.channelRead(ServerHandler.java:77) at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:356) at io.netty.handler.codec.MessageToMessageDecoder.channelRead(MessageToMessageDecoder.java:107) at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:356) at io.netty.channel.CombinedChannelDuplexHandler$DelegatingChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(CombinedChannelDuplexHandler.java:434) at io.netty.handler.codec.ByteToMessageDecoder.fireChannelRead(ByteToMessageDecoder.java:361) at io.netty.handler.codec.ByteToMessageDecoder.channelRead(ByteToMessageDecoder.java:325) at io.netty.channel.CombinedChannelDuplexHandler.channelRead(CombinedChannelDuplexHandler.java:249) at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:354) at io.netty.handler.timeout.IdleStateHandler.channelRead(IdleStateHandler.java:293) at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(AbstractChannelHandlerContext.java:354) at io.netty.channel.DefaultChannelPipeline$HeadContext.channelRead(DefaultChannelPipeline.java:1429) at io.netty.channel.DefaultChannelPipeline.fireChannelRead(DefaultChannelPipeline.java:918) at io.netty.channel.nio.AbstractNioByteChannel$NioByteUnsafe.read(AbstractNioByteChannel.java:172) at io.netty.channel.nio.AbstractNioChannel$AbstractNioUnsafe.handle(AbstractNioChannel.java:445) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler$DefaultNioRegistration.handle(NioIoHandler.java:388) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKey(NioIoHandler.java:596) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKeysOptimized(NioIoHandler.java:571) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKeys(NioIoHandler.java:512) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.run(NioIoHandler.java:484) at io.netty.channel.SingleThreadIoEventLoop.runIo(SingleThreadIoEventLoop.java:225) at io.netty.channel.SingleThreadIoEventLoop.run(SingleThreadIoEventLoop.java:196) at io.netty.util.concurrent.SingleThreadEventExecutor$5.run(SingleThreadEventExecutor.java:1195) at io.netty.util.internal.ThreadExecutorMap$2.run(ThreadExecutorMap.java:74) at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30) at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1119)
kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.
kw.status: StaleElementReferenceException: Message: Cached elements 'By.xpath: //android.widget.EditText' do not exist in DOM anymore; For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#staleelementreferenceexception Stacktrace: io.appium.uiautomator2.common.exceptions.StaleElementReferenceException: Cached elements 'By.xpath: //android.widget.EditText' do not exist in DOM anymore at io.appium.uiautomator2.model.ElementsCache.restore(ElementsCache.java:78) at io.appium.uiautomator2.model.ElementsCache.get(ElementsCache.java:155) at io.appium.uiautomator2.handler.SendKeysToElement.safeHandle(SendKeysToElement.java:104) at io.appium.uiautomator2.handler.request.SafeRequestHandler.handle(SafeRequestHandler.java:59) at io.appium.uiautomator2.server.AppiumServlet.handleRequest(AppiumServlet.java:267) at io.appium.uiautomator2.server.AppiumServlet.handleHttpRequest(AppiumServlet.java:261) at io.appium.uiautomator2.http.ServerHandler.channelRead(ServerHandler.java:77) at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(Abstract... [ Message content over the limit has been removed. ] ...io.AbstractNioChannel$AbstractNioUnsafe.handle(AbstractNioChannel.java:445) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler$DefaultNioRegistration.handle(NioIoHandler.java:388) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKey(NioIoHandler.java:596) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKeysOptimized(NioIoHandler.java:571) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKeys(NioIoHandler.java:512) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.run(NioIoHandler.java:484) at io.netty.channel.SingleThreadIoEventLoop.runIo(SingleThreadIoEventLoop.java:225) at io.netty.channel.SingleThreadIoEventLoop.run(SingleThreadIoEventLoop.java:196) at io.netty.util.concurrent.SingleThreadEventExecutor$5.run(SingleThreadEventExecutor.java:1195) at io.netty.util.internal.ThreadExecutorMap$2.run(ThreadExecutorMap.java:74) at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30) at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1119)

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Clicks the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Element Should Be Visible
kw.msg: Keyword 'AppiumLibrary.Element Should Be Visible' is deprecated. Use `Expect Element` instead Verifies that the element identified by ``locator`` is visible.
kw.doc: *DEPRECATED!!* Use `Expect Element` instead Verifies that the element identified by ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Clicks the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.doc: Clicks the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Clicks the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible

kw.name: Element Should Be Visible
kw.msg: Keyword 'AppiumLibrary.Element Should Be Visible' is deprecated. Use `Expect Element` instead Verifies that the element identified by ``locator`` is visible.
kw.doc: *DEPRECATED!!* Use `Expect Element` instead Verifies that the element identified by ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Close Application
kw.doc: Closes the current application and the webdriver session.

test.doc:
During the 60-minute blocking period, any Forgot Password attempt with the blocked email must display: "You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in X mins." Each retry must show an updated remaining time.
Pre-conditions: FP_BLOCK_EMAIL is currently blocked (run TC_M2.2_026/027 first).
test.status: StaleElementReferenceException: Message: Cached elements 'By.xpath: //android.widget.EditText' do not exist in DOM anymore; For documentation on this error, please visit: https://www.selenium.dev/documentation/webdriver/troubleshooting/errors#staleelementreferenceexception Stacktrace: io.appium.uiautomator2.common.exceptions.StaleElementReferenceException: Cached elements 'By.xpath: //android.widget.EditText' do not exist in DOM anymore at io.appium.uiautomator2.model.ElementsCache.restore(ElementsCache.java:78) at io.appium.uiautomator2.model.ElementsCache.get(ElementsCache.java:155) at io.appium.uiautomator2.handler.SendKeysToElement.safeHandle(SendKeysToElement.java:104) at io.appium.uiautomator2.handler.request.SafeRequestHandler.handle(SafeRequestHandler.java:59) at io.appium.uiautomator2.server.AppiumServlet.handleRequest(AppiumServlet.java:267) at io.appium.uiautomator2.server.AppiumServlet.handleHttpRequest(AppiumServlet.java:261) at io.appium.uiautomator2.http.ServerHandler.channelRead(ServerHandler.java:77) at io.netty.channel.AbstractChannelHandlerContext.fireChannelRead(Abstract... [ Message content over the limit has been removed. ] ...io.AbstractNioChannel$AbstractNioUnsafe.handle(AbstractNioChannel.java:445) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler$DefaultNioRegistration.handle(NioIoHandler.java:388) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKey(NioIoHandler.java:596) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKeysOptimized(NioIoHandler.java:571) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.processSelectedKeys(NioIoHandler.java:512) at io.netty.channel.nio.NioIoHandler.run(NioIoHandler.java:484) at io.netty.channel.SingleThreadIoEventLoop.runIo(SingleThreadIoEventLoop.java:225) at io.netty.channel.SingleThreadIoEventLoop.run(SingleThreadIoEventLoop.java:196) at io.netty.util.concurrent.SingleThreadEventExecutor$5.run(SingleThreadEventExecutor.java:1195) at io.netty.util.internal.ThreadExecutorMap$2.run(ThreadExecutorMap.java:74) at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30) at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1119)

test.name: TC_M2.2_008 - Fifth Failed OTP Attempt Triggers Max Attempts Error And Redirects To FP Email Screen

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Input Text
kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Input Text
kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Input Text
kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.TextView[contains(@text,'maximum number of attempts') or contains(@text,'Verification failed') or contains(@text,'Verification Failed')]
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Expect Element
kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.TextView[contains(@text,'maximum number of attempts') or contains(@text,'Verification failed') or contains(@text,'Verification Failed')]
kw.doc: Verifies that the element with the given ``locator`` has the desired ``state`` (visible, not visible, enabled, disabled.)

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
Wait Until Element Is Visible
Run Keyword And Return Status
Capture Page Screenshot
kw.doc: Takes a screenshot of the current page and embeds it into the log.
Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Could not capture screenshot — no active Appium session
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.msg: xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok'] xpath criteria: //android.widget.Button[@text='Ok']
Element locator 'xpath=//android.widget.Button[@text='Ok']' did not match any elements after 5 seconds
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.
kw.status: Element locator 'xpath=//android.widget.Button[@text='Ok']' did not match any elements after 5 seconds

kw.doc: Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element specified by the ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Expect Element
kw.doc: Verifies that the element with the given ``locator`` has the desired ``state`` (visible, not visible, enabled, disabled.)

kw.name: Close Application
kw.doc: Closes the current application and the webdriver session.

test.doc: Verify that 5 consecutive incorrect OTP entries on the FP screen trigger the max attempts error, then redirect the user back to the FP email screen (m2.2.11) Expected error: "Verification Failed: You have reached the maximum number of attempts..."
